_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: 3U CubeSat primary frame, deployer rails, board stack support
physics_paths: Load Path: Structural loads from solar panels and antennas are transferred through the frame to the base.
Flow Path: Electrical signals travel through the PC/104 stack and connectors.
Thermal Path: Heat generated by electronics is dissipated through the aluminum frame.
Motion Path: Deployable rail guides allow for the extension and retraction of the satellite.
Pressure Path: The vacuum of space exerts uniform pressure on the exterior of the frame.
Human/Service Access Path: Internal components can be accessed via removable panels.
integration_opportunities: Integration of deployable rail guides with the base frame.
Integration of PC/104 standoffs with the electronic boards.
Integration of corner posts with solar panel mounts.
Integration of electronic access panels with the frame shell.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Cold gas propulsion thruster bracket, 4 thrusters cross-pattern, 50mN class, plumbing manifold
physics_paths: Load path: Thrust force from cold gas thrusters transferred through bracket to CubeSat structure.
Flow path: Propellant gas flows through plumbing manifold to thrusters.
Thermal path: Heat generated by thrusters dissipated through bracket to outer surface.
Motion path: Bracket remains static during thruster operation.
Pressure path: Internal pressure within plumbing manifold.
Electrical/Data path: Electrical connections for thruster control.
Human/Service access path: Access for maintenance and installation.
integration_opportunities: Optimize bracket design for weight reduction while maintaining structural integrity.
Integrate additional sensors for health monitoring.
Enhance thermal management to improve efficiency.
Optimize plumbing manifold for reduced pressure drop.

generated_parts.label: Integration Harness + Datum Spine
generated_parts.spec: Integration_harness closure spine with harness_channel, connector_keying, assembly_datum pads, routing clips, service hatch, reciprocal interface labels, inspection checklist marks

requirements: Inter-board harness routing, connectors, assembly datums, connection types and load-path closure
Good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown.
Good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
Good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover.
Good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
Good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating.
Good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled.
Good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances.
Good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums.
Good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance.
Good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters).
Good: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review.
Good: Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops.

verify.value: All required fields present
verify.rule: Ensure all necessary fields are included in the design brief.
Ensure the estimated mass does not exceed the allocated budget.

verify.value: All required features are present in the geometry
verify.rule: Each required feature from the design brief must be represented in the geometry.

verify.value: All interfaces are reciprocal
verify.rule: Interfaces should be reciprocal, meaning the neighboring subsystem can declare the same joint family back.

verify.value: Visible locking evidence on critical joints
verify.rule: Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

risk.mit: Increase channel size or add strain relief features

risk.desc: Service hatch may not provide sufficient access for all components
risk.mit: Review hatch size and location to ensure adequate access

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion causing connector misalignment
risk.mit: Use thermal expansion compensation techniques or materials

risk.desc: Potential for manufacturing defects in CNC process
risk.mit: Conduct thorough inspection and testing post-manufacture
